details.text: Derivative: كُرْهًا from كَرِهَ – كَراهَة which means to dislike or detest. كُرْه refers here to hardship, suffering, fatigues and pains endured by the mother during pregnancy and birth.

Derivative: يَسْتَغِيثَانِ – “They (i.e., the parents) both implore”. From اِسْتَغَاثَ which means to call and appeal for help in the time of dire need or when the consequences of not getting help at that time are terrible.
this verse is identical to the beginning of the previous surah: al-jathiyah.

Derivative: تَجْهَلُونَ implies insolence, being driven and overwhelmed by emotions and refuse to have civil conversation. تَجْهَلُونَ is from جَهِلَ - جَهْلٌ which means to be ignorant and is the opposite of عِلْم (knowledge).
Derivative: صَرَّفْنَا means to present or provide in various and alternative ways.
Derivative: أَنْصِتُوا from أَنْصَتَ and its meaning combines both the meanings of being quiet and listening. أَنْصِتُوا (the plural command form) therefore means: “Be quiet and listen”.
Derivative: أَثَارَةٍ is a trace or remnant of what was left by those who came before i.e., past generations.

Derivative: تُفِيضُونَ from أَفاضَ which means to pour out and implies a large amount. Here it is referring to what pours out of their mouths concerning the Qur’an. It is when people speak a lot and make accusations without thought or evidence. In common day language it is like the expression to run their mouths.
Derivative: بِدْعًا is a created new thing or newly innovated/invented matter.
